result.rationale: Both J-002 confirm-concern (bare first/last name tokens) and J-008 (GitHub handle) are addressed at the correct layer (_scrub in scripts/capture_transcripts.py) with a sound approach: per-token name redaction (len>=4, no leading \b for JSON-\n-glued names, trailing \b protecting "Joneses", re.IGNORECASE) plus handle redaction with stem-minus-"the" and separator-flexible matching ([\s._-]* for len>=8) that catches the spaced source brand without over-redacting common single tokens (whole ordered sequence required; separator class limited to space/._-). I independently grep-verified the committed corpus CASE-INSENSITIVELY: 0 files for word-bound '[REDACTED]', 0 for '[REDACTED]', no standalone '[REDACTED]', no '[REDACTED]@'/'[REDACTED]-', and no genuine spaced '[REDACTED]' brand. The only hits were (a) a single `hipster…ciso` literal and 7 truncated `thehipster…` fragments, all inside THIS/prior review sessions' own grep-command meta-discussion (the prompt explicitly excludes these as non-PII), and (b) coincidental 'thom' substrings in random alphanumeric tokens and 'tHomepage' with no word boundary. 226,337 [REDACTED] markers across 2612 files confirm the corpus was actually re-scrubbed. All 13 tests pass and I directly confirmed the patterns discriminate (name token, handle stem, and spaced brand all redact; 'Joneses' protected; 'the ninja stays' not over-redacted) — they would fail if reverted. No new reconstructable operator-identity vector exists.

Independently verified on branch harden/pass-7 (HEAD bdc2dfc). Root cause and layer are correct: the username/email rules left the operator's bare display-name tokens and GitHub handle in cleartext, and the fix adds (a) a per-name-token redaction in _display_name_patterns() (re.escape(t)+\b, len>=4, re.IGNORECASE, no leading \b for JSON-\n-glued names, trailing \b protecting 'Joneses'), and (b) a new _repo_handle_patterns() deriving the owner from git remote -v + ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES, expanding to the 'the'-stripped stem, and for len>=8 using a separator-flexible [\s._-]* char-join so de-spaced/spaced/dotted/hyphenated brand forms all redact while the whole ordered letter sequence is required (so 'CISO' alone is NOT over-redacted). Both applied in _scrub after the username rule. Corpus is actually clean: case-insensitive greps for '[REDACTED]' (word), '[REDACTED]', standalone '[REDACTED]', '[REDACTED]@'/'[REDACTED]-', spaced 'hipster[sep]ciso', full '[REDACTED][sep][REDACTED]' all return 0/empty; email stems are uniformly masked to [REDACTED]@protonmail (no reconstructable address). Every residual 'thehipster…' fragment (7 files), bare 'hipster' word (3 files), and 'hipster…ciso'/'hipster\nCISO' hit is this session's own scrubbing meta-discussion, a redaction-ellipsis artifact, or a non-contiguous cross-line coincidence — none re-identify the operator and all fall under the explicitly-excluded cases. Over-redaction is bounded and acceptable: standalone 'ciso' (14 files) and 'protonmail' (18 files) are correctly preserved, and the corpus contains no unrelated bearer of the name tokens. Tests: 13 passed; test_scrub_redacts_bare_name_tokens and test_scrub_redacts_github_handle both assert the new behavior (token redaction + Joneses protection + lowercase actor tokens; handle + stem + spaced-brand redaction + no-over-redaction of common tokens) and would fail if the respective code paths were reverted — they are discriminating.
